mn49:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn49:10.1: There is, Brahmā, another host that you do not know or see;
mn49:10.10: Even so, Brahmā, I am not your equal in direct knowledge, much less your inferior.
mn49:10.11: Rather, I am greater than you.
mn49:10.2: I know it and see it.
mn49:10.3: There is, Brahmā, a host called Ābhassara, from which you passed away and reappeared here.
mn49:10.4: Because you dwelt there for so long, that memory has been lost to you; therefore you do not know it or see it.
mn49:10.5: But I know it and see it.
mn49:10.6: Even so, Brahmā, I am not your equal in direct knowledge, much less your inferior.
mn49:10.7: Rather, I am greater than you.
mn49:10.8: There is, Brahmā, a host called Subhakiṇha, a host called Vehapphala, a host called Abhibhū, which you do not know or see.
mn49:10.9: I know it and see it.
mn49:11.1: As to earth, Brahmā, having directly known earth as earth, and having directly known whatever is unexperienced by earth in its earthhood, I did not claim to be earth, I did not claim to be in earth, I did not claim to be apart from earth, I did not claim ‘earth is mine,’ I did not affirm earth.
mn49:11.2: Even so, Brahmā, I am not your equal in direct knowledge, much less your inferior.
mn49:11.3: Rather, I am greater than you.
mn49:2.1: “*Bhikkhus*, at one time I was dwelling at Ukkaṭṭhā, in the Subhaga grove, at the root of a royal sal tree.
mn49:2.2: Now at that time, *bhikkhus*, this evil view had arisen in Baka Brahmā:
mn49:2.3: ‘This is permanent, this is stable, this is eternal, this is complete, this is not liable to passing away; for here one is not born, does not age, does not die, does not pass away, and does not reappear; and beyond this there is no higher escape.’
mn49:23.1: As to the all, Brahmā, having directly known the all as all, and having directly known whatever is unexperienced by the all in its allness, I did not claim to be the all, I did not claim to be in the all, I did not claim to be apart from the all, I did not claim ‘the all is mine,’ I did not affirm the all.
mn49:23.2: Even so, Brahmā, I am not your equal in direct knowledge, much less your inferior.
mn49:23.3: Rather, I am greater than you.’
mn49:24.1: ‘If, good sir, having directly known whatever is unexperienced by the all in its allness, may it not turn out to be merely empty for you, merely hollow for you.’
mn49:25.3: That is unexperienced by earth in its earthhood, unexperienced by water in its waterhood, unexperienced by fire in its firehood, unexperienced by air in its airhood, unexperienced by beings in their beinghood, unexperienced by gods in their godhood, unexperienced by Pajāpati in his Pajāpati-hood, unexperienced by Brahmās in their Brahmā-hood, unexperienced by the Ābhassaras in their Ābhassara-hood, unexperienced by the Subhakiṇhas in their Subhakiṇha-hood, unexperienced by the Vehapphalas in their Vehapphala-hood, unexperienced by Abhibhū in his Abhibhū-hood, unexperienced by the all in its allness.
mn49:26.1: Come then now, good sir, see me disappear.’
mn49:26.10: Having disappeared, I spoke this verse:
mn49:26.2: ‘Come then, Brahmā, you disappear from me, if you can.’
mn49:26.4: ‘I shall disappear from the ascetic Gotama, I shall disappear from the ascetic Gotama,’ but he was unable to disappear from me.
mn49:26.5: When this was said, *bhikkhus*, I said to Baka Brahmā:
mn49:26.6: ‘Come then now, Brahmā, see me disappear.’
mn49:26.7: ‘Come then, good sir, disappear from me, if you can.’
mn49:26.8: Then, *bhikkhus*, I performed such a feat of psychic power that I resolved:
mn49:26.9: ‘So far as Brahmā and Brahmā’s assembly and Brahmā’s attendants are concerned, they will hear my voice, but they will not see me.’
mn49:27.2: and also in those seeking non-*bhava*;
mn49:27.3: I did not affirm any *bhava* at all,
mn49:27.4: nor did I cling to delight.’
mn49:28.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, Brahmā and Brahmā’s assembly and Brahmā’s attendants became filled with amazement and wonder:
mn49:28.2: ‘It is wonderful, sirs, it is astonishing, sirs!
mn49:28.3: How great is the might and power of the ascetic Gotama! Never before have we seen or heard of another recluse or brahmin so mighty and so powerful as this ascetic Gotama, the Sakyan son who went forth from a Sakyan clan.
mn49:28.4: Though this generation delights in *bhava*, revels in *bhava*, rejoices in *bhava*, he has uprooted *bhava* down to the root.’
mn49:29.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, Māra the Evil One, having entered one of Brahmā’s attendants, said this to me:
mn49:29.10: at the breakup of the body, at the cutting off of life, they became established in a refined body.
mn49:29.12: come then, good sir, dwell at ease, devoted to a pleasant abiding here and now. For the wholesome is undeclared, good sir; do not instruct another.’
mn49:29.2: ‘If, good sir, you understand thus, if you have awakened to this, then do not lead disciples or those gone forth.
mn49:29.3: Do not teach *dhamma* to disciples or to those gone forth.
mn49:29.4: Do not make attachment to disciples or to those gone forth.
mn49:29.5: There were, *bhikkhu*, recluses and brahmins in the world before you claiming to be *arahants*, perfectly awakened ones.
mn49:29.6: They led disciples and those gone forth, they taught *dhamma* to disciples and to those gone forth, they made attachment to disciples and to those gone forth; having led disciples and those gone forth, having taught *dhamma* to disciples and to those gone forth, with minds attached to disciples and to those gone forth,
mn49:29.7: at the breakup of the body, at the cutting off of life, they became established in an inferior body.
mn49:29.8: But there were, *bhikkhu*, recluses and brahmins in the world before you claiming to be *arahants*, perfectly awakened ones.
mn49:29.9: They did not lead disciples or those gone forth, they did not teach *dhamma* to disciples or to those gone forth, they did not make attachment to disciples or to those gone forth; having not led disciples or those gone forth, having not taught *dhamma* to disciples or to those gone forth, with minds not attached to disciples or to those gone forth,
mn49:3.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, knowing with my mind the thought in Baka Brahmā’s mind—
mn49:3.2: just as a strong man might extend his bent arm or bend his extended arm, so too I vanished from Ukkaṭṭhā, from the Subhaga grove at the root of a royal sal tree, and appeared in that Brahmā world.
mn49:3.3: Then, *bhikkhus*, Baka Brahmā saw me coming in the distance.
mn49:3.6: It is after a long time, good sir, that you have made this occasion, that is, for coming here.
mn49:3.7: For this is permanent, this is stable, this is eternal, this is complete, this is not liable to passing away; for here one is not born, does not age, does not die, does not pass away, and does not reappear. And beyond this there is no higher escape.’
mn49:30.1: When this was said, *bhikkhus*, I said to Māra the Evil One:
mn49:30.10: But I, Evil One, being truly perfectly awakened, claim: “I am perfectly awakened.”
mn49:30.11: For whether the Tathāgata teaches *dhamma* to disciples or does not teach *dhamma* to disciples, he is just the same.
mn49:30.12: And whether the Tathāgata leads disciples or does not lead disciples, he is just the same.
mn49:30.14: Because, Evil One, those *āsavas* of the Tathāgata that are defiling, leading to renewed *bhava* [*ponobbhavikā*], bringing distress, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, aging, and death—
mn49:30.15: those have been abandoned, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, made into nonexistence, not subject to arising in the future.
mn49:30.16: Just as a palm tree with its crown cut off is incapable of growing again,
mn49:30.17: so too, Evil One, those *āsavas* of the Tathāgata that are defiling, leading to renewed *bhava* [*ponobbhavikā*], bringing distress, ripening in *dukkha*, and leading onward to future birth, aging, and death—
mn49:30.18: those have been abandoned, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, made into nonexistence, not subject to arising in the future.’
mn49:30.2: ‘I know you, Evil One; do not think:
mn49:30.5: It is not out of compassion for my welfare, Evil One, that you speak thus to me.
mn49:30.6: It is out of compassion for my harm, Evil One, that you speak thus to me.
mn49:30.7: For you, Evil One, think thus:
mn49:30.8: “Those to whom the ascetic Gotama will teach *dhamma* will pass beyond my domain.”
mn49:30.9: Though not perfectly awakened, those ones, Evil One, claimed: “We are perfectly awakened.”
mn49:31.1: Thus, because of Māra’s silence and because of Brahmā’s invitation, this exposition is therefore called “Brahmā’s Invitation.”
mn49:31.2: The ninth is finished: Brahmā’s Invitation.
mn49:4.1: When this was said, *bhikkhus*, I said to Baka Brahmā:
mn49:4.2: ‘Baka Brahmā is indeed overcome by blindness [*avijjā*], sir, Baka Brahmā is indeed overcome by blindness [*avijjā*], sir,
mn49:4.3: inasmuch as he will say of what is merely impermanent [*aniccaṁ*] that it is permanent, of what is unstable that it is stable, of what is non-eternal that it is eternal, of what is incomplete that it is complete, of what is liable to passing away that it is not liable to passing away.
mn49:4.4: And where there is birth, aging, dying, passing away, and reappearing, of that he says:
mn49:4.5: “For here one is not born, does not age, does not die, does not pass away, and does not reappear.”
mn49:4.6: And though there is another higher escape, he says: “There is no other higher escape.”’
mn49:5.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, Māra the Evil One, having entered one of Brahmā’s attendants, said this to me:
mn49:5.10: Come then, good sir, do just what Brahmā tells you; do not overstep Brahmā’s word.
mn49:5.11: Do you not see Brahmā’s assembly gathered here?’
mn49:5.12: Thus, *bhikkhus*, Māra the Evil One brought Brahmā’s assembly to bear on me.
mn49:5.2: ‘*Bhikkhu*, *bhikkhu*, do not assail him, do not assail him. For this, *bhikkhu*, is Brahmā, the Great Brahmā, the conqueror, the unconquered, the all-seeing, the wielder of mastery, the lord, the maker, the creator, the chief, the appointor, the master, the father of those who have been and those who will be.
mn49:5.3: Those recluses and brahmins in the world before you, *bhikkhu*, who reviled earth and were disgusted with earth, who reviled water and were disgusted with water, who reviled fire and were disgusted with fire, who reviled air and were disgusted with air, who reviled beings and were disgusted with beings, who reviled gods and were disgusted with gods, who reviled Pajāpati and were disgusted with Pajāpati, who reviled Brahmā and were disgusted with Brahmā—
mn49:5.4: at the breakup of the body, at the cutting off of life, they became established in an inferior body.
mn49:5.5: But those recluses and brahmins in the world before you, *bhikkhu*, who praised earth and delighted in earth, who praised water and delighted in water, who praised fire and delighted in fire, who praised air and delighted in air, who praised beings and delighted in beings, who praised gods and delighted in gods, who praised Pajāpati and delighted in Pajāpati, who praised Brahmā and delighted in Brahmā—
mn49:5.6: at the breakup of the body, at the cutting off of life, they became established in a refined body.
mn49:5.7: So I say to you, *bhikkhu*, this:
mn49:5.8: “Come then, good sir, do just what Brahmā tells you; do not overstep Brahmā’s word.”
mn49:5.9: If you overstep Brahmā’s word, *bhikkhu*, it will be as if a man, when good fortune came to him, were to beat it away with a stick; or as if a man falling into a hellish abyss were to miss the earth with his hands and feet. Just so, *bhikkhu*, that will be your outcome.
mn49:6.1: When this was said, *bhikkhus*, I said to Māra the Evil One:
mn49:6.2: ‘I know you, Evil One; do not think:
mn49:6.5: And that Brahmā, Evil One, and Brahmā’s assembly, and Brahmā’s attendants—they are all in your grasp, they are all under your power.
mn49:6.6: For you, Evil One, think thus:
mn49:6.7: “He too would be in my grasp; he too would be under my power.”
mn49:6.8: But I, Evil One, am neither in your grasp nor under your power.’
mn49:7.1: When this was said, *bhikkhus*, Baka Brahmā said this to me:
mn49:7.16: if you cling to Brahmā, you will come within my sphere, established on my ground, to be dealt with as I wish, subject to my will.”’
mn49:7.2: ‘For I, good sir, speak of what is indeed permanent as “permanent,” I speak of what is indeed stable as “stable,” I speak of what is indeed eternal as “eternal,” I speak of what is indeed complete as “complete,” I speak of what is indeed not liable to passing away as “not liable to passing away”; and where there is no birth, no aging, no dying, no passing away, no reappearing, that is what I say:
mn49:7.3: “For here one is not born, does not age, does not die, does not pass away, and does not reappear.”
mn49:7.4: And where there is no other higher escape, that is what I say: “There is no other higher escape.”
mn49:7.5: Those recluses and brahmins in the world before you, *bhikkhu*—their ascetic practice lasted only as long as your full lifespan.
mn49:7.6: If there were another higher escape, they would understand: “There is another higher escape”; or if there were no other higher escape, they would understand: “There is no other higher escape.”
mn49:7.7: So I say to you, *bhikkhu*, this:
mn49:7.8: “You will certainly not see another higher escape, and you will only become a sharer in weariness and vexation.
mn49:7.9: If you cling to earth, you will come within my sphere, established on my ground, to be dealt with as I wish, subject to my will.
mn49:8.10: “So mighty is Baka Brahmā, so powerful is Baka Brahmā, so glorious is Baka Brahmā.”
mn49:8.11: But how, good sir, do you understand my range and my radiance:
mn49:8.12: “So mighty is Baka Brahmā, so powerful is Baka Brahmā, so glorious is Baka Brahmā”?’
mn49:8.2: “If I cling to earth, I would come within your sphere, established on your ground, to be dealt with as you wish, subject to your will.
mn49:8.9: if I cling to Brahmā, I would come within your sphere, established on your ground, to be dealt with as you wish, subject to your will.” Yet, Brahmā, I understand your range, and I understand your radiance: “So mighty is Baka Brahmā, so powerful is Baka Brahmā, so glorious is Baka Brahmā.”
mn49:9.10: “So mighty is Baka Brahmā, so powerful is Baka Brahmā, so glorious is Baka Brahmā.”
mn49:9.2: move about, shining and illuminating the directions,
mn49:9.3: so far extends the thousandfold world—
mn49:9.5: You know what is above and below,
mn49:9.6: and also those with passion and free of passion;
mn49:9.7: the state of being thus and otherwise,
mn49:9.8: the coming and going of beings.
mn49:9.9: Thus, Brahmā, I understand your range, and I understand your radiance:
